# Automation Checklist: Comfygo Patched Tmux Control

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of requirements for the sequential launch automation, orchestration flow (update/verify/tmux), settings-driven behavior, tmux control surface, and cross-story integration points (patching + enrichment) in the Comfygo Patched Tmux Control feature.
**Created**: 2026-06-21
**Feature**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It tests the requirements themselves for completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and coverage - not the implementation.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the exact ordered phases of the primary launch command (bring base up to date using vendored + patches, run verification steps, launch inside tmux window/pane) explicitly required? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, US2]
- [ ] CHK002 Is up-front declarative settings loading required to occur before any update, patch, verify, or launch action in the automation sequence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, FR-002, Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK003 Are requirements for post-launch control (ability to send status/stop/restart commands from the original controlling shell to the tmux target) fully specified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, US2]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements for embedding patching (US1 re-patch using manifests) as part of the "get everything up to date" phase of launch defined? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, FR-004, US1]
- [ ] CHK005 Are integration points for model enrichment (US3: HF/Civitai/JSON) into the launch automation or as explicit subcommand specified? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, US3, Phase 5]
- [ ] CHK006 Is support for both git-cloned HF full folders and loose files required within any automation that touches models? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, US3]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is the tmux target management (window name via COMFYGO_LAUNCH_TMUX_NAME, use of `new-window`, pane vs window semantics, attach vs new) unambiguously described? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities §Tmux Target, Clarifications]
- [ ] CHK008 Are the precise effects of individual COMFYGO_* settings keys (e.g. COMFYGO_LAUNCH_TMUX_NAME, COMFYGO_PATCH_POLICY, COMFYGO_ENRICH_CIVITAI, COMFYGO_PROTECTION_REMIND) on the launch sequence enumerated? [Clarity, Spec §Key Entities §Up-front Settings]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "verification steps" in the required sequence (FR-002) defined with enough specificity (doctor, protection, node presence, drift checks)? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002, US2]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the behavior when settings are missing or partial (safe defaults + clear user guidance) specified in the automation flow? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK011 Is "single launch command" quantified (what counts as one command vs multi-step manual) and is coexistence with direct comfy-cli calls addressed? [Clarity, Spec §US2, Clarifications]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do the automation/launch requirements align with the "vendored repo is source of truth" for patching and the explicit refresh only principle? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Constitution I, II]
- [ ] CHK013 Are the SSH + direnv-only constraints (no X11/GUI) consistently required for all automation flows including patching, launch, re-patch, and enrichment? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, SC-004]
- [ ] CHK014 Are privacy constraints (no secrets, no private paths, no usage-revealing model names in committed manifests/docs/issues) applied uniformly to automation artifacts and examples? [Consistency, Spec §FR-011, SC-005, Constitution V]
- [ ] CHK015 Do requirements for preserving existing speckit artifacts (doctor, verify, descriptor/registry, GC harnesses) apply to any new automation entry points and docs? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, SC-007]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can SC-001 (one command → fully patched/verified/tmux-launched while retaining interactive controlling shell, no manual comfy-cli after settings) be objectively measured from the stated requirements? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, US2]
- [ ] CHK017 Are SC-002 re-patch outcomes after comfy update measurable within the context of an automated launch sequence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK018 Is SC-004 (all primary flows over plain SSH + direnv) expressed with verifiable success conditions for the full automation sequence? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK019 Can "the app can still drive the tmuxed process" (status, stop) be verified against explicit requirements without relying on implementation details? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US2, FR-003]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are requirements defined for re-invoking the launch automation while a tmux-launched ComfyUI is already running? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK021 Are control command scenarios (status/stop from controlling shell to active tmux target) covered as part of the automation contract? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §US2, FR-003]
- [ ] CHK022 Are scenarios for enrichment during or after launch automation (or as separate step) with Civitai/HF success and failure addressed? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §US3, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements specified for the full sequence executing over plain SSH single-terminal with direnv-loaded tokens only? [Scenario Coverage, Spec §SC-004, FR-010]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK024 Is fallback or clear error + hint required when tmux is absent on the server during automation launch? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK025 Are requirements for handling partial failures in the sequence (e.g. verify fails, patch mismatch, enrichment API fails) specified (fail-fast, continue with warning, or configurable)? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK026 Is re-patch during an active tmux session (without disrupting running ComfyUI unless told) required? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK027 Are malformed or incomplete up-front settings handled with safe defaults and guidance without leaking secrets? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, FR-011]

## Non-Functional Requirements

- [ ] CHK028 Are uv-first constraints explicitly required for all new or modified automation scripts, wrappers, and Python helpers? [Non-Functional, Constitution VI, Repository Policy]
- [ ] CHK029 Are branch protection and safety net (Codacy, changelog) requirements referenced for any automation changes that affect committed flows or docs? [Non-Functional, Constitution VIII, IX]
- [ ] CHK030 Are requirements for verifiable status (doctor, logs, status commands) included for the automation sequence? [Non-Functional, Constitution VII, Spec §FR-007 context]
- [ ] CHK031 Are privacy and secret-safety non-functional requirements carried through to any example commands, logs, or committed automation documentation? [Non-Functional, Spec §SC-005, FR-011]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK032 Are assumptions about direnv presence for tokens and settings, plus tmux availability, documented with validation expectations for the automation? [Dependencies, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK033 Are external service dependencies (Civitai API, HF) with graceful degradation when tokens or connectivity are missing specified for any automated enrichment path? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-008, Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK034 Is preservation of prior speckit artifacts (001-003 GC, doctor, descriptor, .comfygo_views) as non-breaking constraint on new automation explicitly required? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK035 Are the two known patches (comfyui + comfy-cli local-nodes) plus vendored base treated as the minimal sufficient set for automation-driven recreation of desired state? [Dependencies, Spec §FR-012, Assumptions]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Focus: Automation / orchestration / sequential launch + tmux control + settings integration + US1/US3 touchpoints (derived from US2 primary + cross-US requirements)
- Depth: Standard (reviewer/PR)
- This checklist tests whether the automation-related requirements are well-written, complete, unambiguous, and ready for implementation.
- Suggested follow-up: address any [Gap] items before final converge/verify; consider adding explicit sequence error policy and enrichment-in-launch integration requirements if intended.
